topic.name: Temporary Duty Suspensions
topic.description: Legislative amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States for the temporary suspension of duties on various imported goods.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain work footwear for women.
Document 2: temporarily suspend the duty on textile fabrics of man-made fibers impregnated, coated, covered or laminated with polyurethane.
Document 3: extend temporarily the suspension of duty on nylon woolpacks used to package wool.
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on certain women's footwear with outer soles and uppers of rubber or plastics.
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain cases or containers to be used for electronic drawing toys.
Document 6: suspend temporarily the duty on certain synthetic staple fibers that are not carded, combed, or otherwise processed for spinning.
Document 7: extend the temporary suspension of duty on a mixture of barium carbonate, strontium carbonate, calcium carbonate, and 1-methoxy-2-propanol acetate.

topic.name: Small Business Financial Assistance
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at providing financial support and resources to small businesses, particularly in underserved and economically distressed areas.

Document 1: Securing Equity for the Economic Development of Low Income Areas Act of 2009
to select at least one company from each geographic region of the SBA
Document 2: Department of Transportation Bonding Assistance Authority Act of 2009
to prescribe, guarantee, and enter into commitments to guarantee a surety against loss
Document 3: Small Business Emergency Capital Assistance Act of 2009
make loans directly to small businesses determined to be economically healthy
Document 4: Small Business Opportunity Acceleration Act of 2013
eliminate loan guarantee fees for certain small business loans
Document 5: Hawaiian Homeownership Opportunity Act of 2011
extend through FY2015 the authorization of appropriations for housing assistance for Native Americans
Document 6: financial assistance to farm owners through the Farmers Home Administration
demonstrates that it is experiencing economic distress
Document 7: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act
removes the condition that the dwelling be a member's primary residence

topic.name: Data Privacy and Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at regulating the collection, use, and protection of personal data and privacy rights across different contexts, including mobile applications, children's online activities, and journalistic protections.

Document 1: Directs mobile device application developers...to notify the user and obtain the user's consent regarding the terms and conditions governing the collection, use, storage, and sharing of such personal data.
Requires developers to...provide users with a method to withdraw such consent and to request that the developer delete personal data.
Document 3: Prohibits an operator of a website, online service, online application, or mobile application directed to children...from using, disclosing to third parties, or compiling personal information collected from children or minors for targeted marketing purposes.
Requires verifiable parental consent...for the collection, use, or disclosure of personal information of a child.
Document 5: Prohibits a federal entity...from compelling a covered journalist to disclose protected information, unless a U.S. judge...determines...that all reasonable alternative sources have been exhausted.
Defines a 'covered journalist' as a person who...is an employee, independent contractor, or agent of an entity or service that disseminates news or information.

topic.name: Education Funding and Improvement
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at enhancing educational resources, accountability, and support systems for public schools in the United States.

Document 1: Repeals the requirement that the Secretary of Education deny Texas and its local educational agencies funds from the Education Jobs Fund
state funding for elementary and secondary education, as a percentage of the state's total revenue, will not be reduced through FY2013.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Education to make annual determinations as to whether each state's public school system provides all its students with the educational resources to succeed academically
requires withholding of specified portions of its federal funding for administrative expenses if a system fails to meet a yearly interim goal.
Document 3: Provides a moratorium on compliance by failing schools with certain requirements for achieving adequate yearly progress
Requires the Secretary of Education to ensure that negative consequences shall not apply to such agencies for actions taken under this Act.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to make grants to states and, through them, subgrants to local educational agencies (LEAs) for differentiated high school improvement systems
Requires state grantees to establish comprehensive school performance indicators and minimum annual improvement benchmarks.
Document 5: Amends the school improvement program under part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to define 'highly qualified teachers'
Requires LEAs to ensure that poor, minority, disabled, and limited English proficient students are taught by highly qualified and effective teachers at similar rates and ratios as other students.
Document 6: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award renewable, competitive Innovation grants
Includes among those activities: improving instructional programs, ensuring that English learners are taught by effective teachers.
Document 7: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to local educational agencies (LEAs)
Requires each grant applicant to demonstrate that it has partnered with at least one private entity that will provide matching funds.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments and Benefits
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at providing tax benefits, credits, and deductions for individuals and businesses.

Document 1: Imposes a federal income tax rate of 15% on all income of all U.S. citizens
Allows exemptions for single and joint tax return filers and for each child of a taxpayer up to the age of 18.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the maximum dollar limitation on the Hope Scholarship tax credit
Allow such credit for four years of postsecondary education.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a medical care tax deduction for up to $1,000
Defines 'qualified sports and fitness expenses' as amounts paid for fitness facility memberships.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide employers a business tax credit for up to 10% of compensation not paid to their employees
Limits such credit to $2,000 for any one Ready Reserve-National Guard employee per year.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide a one-time increase in the exclusion of gain from the sale of a principal residence
Provides that in the case of a joint return, only one spouse need satisfy the age requirement.
Document 6: Amends Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the tax exclusion for benefits paid to volunteer firefighters
Increase the amount of benefits excludible.
Document 7: Allows a reduction in 2010 and 2011 of an employer's payroll taxes
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the tax credit for increasing research activities.

topic.name: Water and Fisheries Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, conservation, and regulation of water resources and fisheries in the United States.

Document 1: Safe and Secure Drinking Water Act of 2014 - Requires the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to develop and publish within 90 days a health advisory including recommendations on the level of microcystins in drinking water that is safe for human consumption.
Directs the EPA to report within 120 days and every year thereafter on the status of the determination regarding microcystins.
Document 2: Amends the Flood Disaster Protection Act of 1973 to redefine 'private flood insurance' to include a policy issued by an insurance company.
This act relates to the management of flood risks and insurance.
Document 4: This bill amends the Magnuson-Stevens Fishery Conservation and Management Act to revise requirements on fishing and fisheries in the Gulf of Mexico.
State jurisdiction over fisheries in the Gulf of Mexico is expanded to nine nautical miles.
Document 5: This bill amends the Saltonstall-Kennedy Act to revise the program under which the Department of Commerce provides financial assistance for fisheries research and development projects.
The plans must be revised and approved by the relevant council at least once every five years.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish a sediment and nutrient monitoring network for the Upper Mississippi River Basin.
Requires the Director to establish a computer modeling program to identify nutrient and sediment sources in the Basin.

topic.name: Energy Tax Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at amending tax codes related to different energy sources and their environmental impacts.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to expand the definition of 'crude oil' for purposes of the excise tax on petroleum
Authorizes the Secretary of the Treasury to include as crude oil or as a petroleum product for excise tax purposes any fuel feedstock or finished fuel product
Document 2: This bill equalizes the excise tax on liquefied petroleum gas and liquefied natural gas
establishing a rate of 18.3 cents per energy equivalent of a gallon of gasoline for liquefied petroleum gas
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to impose a $10 per ton excise tax on the extraction of coal
establish in the Treasury the Coal Mitigation Trust Fund to hold revenues from such tax
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify the definition of 'refined coal' for purposes of the tax credit for producing electricity from renewable resources
include certain emission reduction certification requirements
Document 5: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the definition of 'renewable biomass'
to include slash and precommercial sized thinnings harvested in environmentally sustainable quantities
Document 6: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the taxpayer election to expense depreciable business property
to make permanent the increased $500,000 limitation on the amount of such property eligible for expensing
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a 30% tax credit for investment in a qualifying offshore wind facility
direct the Secretary of the Treasury to establish a qualifying credit for offshore wind facilities program

topic.name: Child Welfare and Gang Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving child welfare, preventing gang activity, and protecting vulnerable populations.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to define 'criminal street gang' and 'gang crime'
Increases criminal penalties for certain violent crimes in aid of racketeering activity.
Document 2: Directs the Attorney General to establish policies and procedures for a program for national criminal history background checks for child-serving organizations
Limit the liability of a child-serving organization for failure to conduct criminal background checks.
Document 3: Promote the well-being of maltreated infants and toddlers and their families
Help prevent the recurrence of abuse and neglect of children.
Document 4: Require state plans for foster care and adoption assistance to have procedures to assist alien children
Authorize the use of court improvement program grant funds to educate and train child welfare and court staff.
Document 5: Establish an automated National Putative Father Registry
Improve the protection of the rights of responsible unwed fathers in legal proceedings.
Document 6: Reauthorize the Missing Alzheimer's Disease Patient Alert Program
Provide for competitive grants to nonprofit organizations to assist in locating missing patients.
Document 7: Authorize the Attorney General to make grants to public and nonprofit private entities for projects involving innovative approaches to combat gang activity
Reauthorize programs for combating criminal gangs, including after-school programs.

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures and benefits aimed at supporting veterans, their families, and dependents.

Document 1: preference eligible for federal employment purposes the parent of an individual who lost his or her life under honorable conditions while serving in the Armed Forces
service-connected permanently and totally disabled veteran
Document 2: Entitles to educational assistance under the post-9/11 educational assistance program the surviving spouses of members of the Armed Forces who die while serving on active duty
15 years after the member's death to utilize such assistance
Document 3: Permits a veteran with any compensable service-connected disability to use commissary and exchange stores
the veteran's dependents
Document 4: changes the days on which the POW/MIA flag is required to be displayed
all days on which the U.S. flag is displayed
Document 5: payment of a monthly assistance allowance to disabled veterans training or competing for the U.S. Paralympic Team
assistance to the United States Paralympics, Inc.
Document 6: develop a rules-based expert system to automate the adjudication of veterans' disability compensation claims
provide a disability rating recommendation based on the applicable rate of disability compensation
Document 7: authorize a military chaplain to close the prayer according to the dictates of the chaplain's conscience

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Rights
topic.description: The documents collectively address various aspects of abortion laws, including regulations on informed consent, parental notification, and prohibitions against discriminatory practices in abortion services.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to make it an unfair labor practice for an employer to recognize or bargain collectively with a labor organization that has not been selected by a majority of the employees in a secret ballot election.

an employer to recognize or bargain collectively with a labor organization that has not been selected by a majority of the employees in a secret ballot election conducted by the National Labor Relations Board (NLRB)
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require an abortion provider who knowingly performs an abortion of a pain-capable unborn child to first inform the woman of the probable age of the child.
Creates an exception to save the life of the mother.
Document 3: Imposes criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly performs an abortion knowing that the abortion is sought based on the sex or gender of the child.
Defines 'sex-selection abortion' as an abortion undertaken to eliminate an unborn child based on the sex or gender of the child.
Document 4: Amends the Clayton Act to grant the United States exclusive authority to bring suit for injunctive relief against any common carrier that is not a rail common carrier.
Empowers the Federal Trade Commission to regulate, and engage in antitrust enforcement regarding, collective rate agreements and certain transactions.
Document 5: Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion.

Makes it an affirmative defense to a prosecution or civil action under this Act that a defendant reasonably believed that before the minor obtained the abortion, the required parental consent or notification took place.
Document 6: Amends the Privacy Act of 1974 to specify that the liability of the United States in a civil action brought by an individual adversely affected by a federal agency's intentional or willful failure to maintain accurate records concerning the individual is provable damages.
which excludes damages for mental or emotional distress.
Document 7: This bill amends the federal criminal code to make it a crime to have physical custody of a living human embryo and refuse to release the embryo for implantation in a willing implantee at the request of one of the progenitors of that embryo.
A violator is subject to a fine, up to three months in prison, or both.

topic.name: Federal Security and Communication Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at enhancing security and communication protocols within federal agencies and transportation systems.

Document 1: the General Services Administration to procure an antivirus product suitable for federal agencies
provide to individuals whose personally identifiable information may have been compromised
Document 2: prohibits the Secretary of Transportation and the Administrator of the FAA from regulating airline operations
includes actions such as auctioning rights or implementing peak-period pricing
Document 3: requires the FCC to complete a rulemaking for 9-1-1 text message communication
ensures consumers are informed if their text message to 9-1-1 is not received
Document 4: establish federal air cargo screening centers at U.S. airports
ensure air cargo screening is coordinated with established security programs
Document 5: require airport operators to modify security programs for video surveillance systems
prohibits the use of images against airport employees in employment disciplinary matters
Document 6: require insurers to disclose total loss information for passenger vehicles
provide details such as vehicle identification number and reason for total loss
Document 7: require GPS coordinate information to be transmitted periodically
amends regulations regarding flight data recorders

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, preservation, and development of federal and state lands in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Claims Resolution Act of 2010 to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to enter into contracts under the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act with eligible Indian tribes to carry out activities under the Land Consolidation Program.
Makes the Secretary responsible for ensuring that all payments are made to beneficiaries within one year.
Document 2: Amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to designate certain lands in Colorado in the White River National Forest and certain public lands administered by Colorado River Valley Field Office of the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) as wilderness.
Designates certain BLM administered lands and certain lands in the White River National Forest as the Red Table Mountain, Pisgah Mountain, and Castle Peak Areas.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey, without consideration, to the city of Anchorage, Alaska, the reversionary interests of the United States in specified parcels of non-federal land in order to unencumber the title to such land to enable the economic development of such land.
Requires the city to pay all costs associated with such conveyance.
Document 4: Authorizes the Commandant of the Coast Guard to lease tidelands and submerged lands under the control of the Coast Guard for periods longer than five years.
Specified amounts of rental or lease monies received to be deposited in the Treasury.
Document 5: Requires each public building constructed, altered, or acquired by the Administrator of General Services (GSA) to incorporate bird-safe building materials and design features.
Directs the Administrator to address the impacts of interior and exterior lighting on native bird species.
Document 6: Amends the Fur Seal Act of 1966 to revise provisions concerning the disposal of federal property on the Pribilof Islands.
Gives those village corporations the first priority right to acquire that land on their respective islands.
Document 7: Amends the Saguro National Park Establishment Act of 1994 to modify further the boundaries of Saguaro National Park to include specified land.
Directs the Secretary to conduct and report on a study that identifies lands that would be a part of any future boundary adjustments for the Park.

topic.name: Regulatory Oversight and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing regulatory processes, ensuring transparency, and improving accountability within federal agencies.

Document 1: Amends the Regulatory Flexibility Act of 1980 (RFA) to define the 'economic impact' of a proposed or final rule
Requires initial and final regulatory flexibility analyses under RFA
Document 2: Requires the Archivist of the United States to promulgate regulations governing federal agency preservation of electronic messages
Federal agency heads must report to the Archivist on agency compliance with the regulations
Document 3: Prohibits the use of private contractors for the arrest, interrogation, detention, or transportation or transfer of persons under government custody
Requires each assessment to be submitted to the congressional intelligence committees
Document 4: Grants inspectors general additional subpoena authority to compel the attendance and testimony of certain witnesses
Requires the Council to conduct and report on an analysis of critical issues that involve the jurisdiction of more than one individual federal agency or entity
Document 5: Defines 'major rule' and 'major guidance' for purposes of this Act
Revises procedures for rule making under the Administrative Procedure Act (APA)
Document 6: Amends the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act of 1978 (FISA) to modify the purposes for which the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) is authorized to apply to a FISA court
Requires applications for such production orders to include specific and articulable facts showing that there are reasonable grounds
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Defense to ensure that the results of command climate assessments are provided to the relevant individual commander
Requires unit commanders to gather all climate assessments from the unit and develop a compliance report

topic.name: Water and Habitat Management
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at improving water management and habitat conservation efforts across various ecosystems.

Document 1: authorize the Forest Service and the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to enter into stewardship contracting projects
land management goals of a project may include watershed restoration and maintenance
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to implement the Stipulation of Settlement
identify impacts and measures to mitigate impacts on adjacent and downstream water users
Document 3: scientific analysis of federal salmon recovery actions
impact that removal of the four lower Snake River dams would have on recovery of salmon
Document 4: require the Secretary of the Army to provide to the applicant a statement that describes whether the Secretary concurs with the assessment
establish the Corps of Engineers Board of Appeals
Document 5: submit to Congress, make publicly available, and biennially update an Asset Management Report
standardize and streamline data reporting and processes across regions
Document 6: conduct a study of irrigation infrastructure for 18 Rio Grande Pueblos
develop a list of projects recommended to be implemented over a 10-year period
Document 7: establish the National Fish Habitat Board to promote, oversee, and coordinate the implementation of this Act
designate Fish Habitat Partnerships to coordinate the implementation of the National Fish Habitat Action Plan

topic.name: Spectrum Management and Environmental Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to the management of electromagnetic spectrum and environmental regulations concerning greenhouse gas emissions and energy infrastructure.

Document 1: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to require the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to biennially inventory each radio spectrum band
Directs the FCC and NTIA to conduct a study of occupancy on the electromagnetic spectrum.
Document 2: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a federal greenhouse gas (GHG) registry
Defines 'GHG emissions' to mean emissions of a GHG, including stationary combustion source emissions.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Transportation (DOT) to establish the Private-Public Partnership Challenge Grant Program
Authorizes the Secretary to provide grants to states for use in implementing innovative strategies to use private-public partnerships.
Document 4: Authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P. to construct, connect, operate, and maintain the pipeline
Deems the Final Supplemental Environmental Impact Statement regarding the pipeline issued by the Secretary of State in January 2014 to fully satisfy the National Environmental Policy Act.
Document 5: Prohibits the Secretary of the Interior from issuing or approving any proposed or final regulation under the Surface Mining Control and Reclamation Act that would adversely impact employment in coal mines
Amends the Clean Air Act to prohibit the Administrator of the EPA from promulgating any regulation concerning greenhouse gas emissions.
Document 6: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to extend the exemption from retransmission consent requirements for satellite retransmissions
Extends the prohibition on exclusive retransmission consent contracts.
Document 7: Requires the NTIA and FCC to make available at least 255 megahertz of federal and nonfederal spectrum for mobile and fixed wireless broadband use
The NTIA must submit to Congress and the FCC an assessment of the feasibility of authorizing mobile or fixed terrestrial wireless operations.

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Amendments
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative amendments and acts related to tariffs, transportation, fisheries management, and education policies in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain ice cream makers.
suspend temporarily the duty on certain tamper resistant ground fault circuit interrupters.
Document 3: Extends and modifies the temporary suspension of duty on certain men's footwear.
designed to be worn in lieu of, but not over, other footwear as a protection against water, oil, grease, or chemicals or cold or inclement weather.
Document 4: Moving Obstructed Trains In-between Openings Now (MOTION) Act
to remain or go on duty for a period in excess of the limitations established under hours-of-service requirements.
Document 5: West Coast Dungeness Crab Management Act
to adopt and enforce state laws governing fishing and processing in the exclusive economic zone.
Document 6: Schoolchildren's Health Protection Act
to prohibit the provision of federal education funding to state or local educational agencies.
Document 7: Amends the Death on the High Seas Act
to make provisions concerning commercial aviation accidents also applicable to maritime accidents.

topic.name: Social Security and Disability Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts that amend the Social Security Act and related programs to improve benefits and access for individuals with disabilities and specific demographic groups.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Labor to award grants to states to pay for the federal share of the cost of carrying out programs that assist families
wage replacement for eligible individuals taking leave to respond to caregiving needs
Document 2: Directs the Commissioner of Social Security to amend specified Listings of Impairments
waive the 24-month waiting period for coverage under the Medicare program for individuals diagnosed with Huntington's Disease
Document 3: eliminate the two-year waiting period following a divorce for a divorced spouse to become entitled to a spouse's OASDI benefits
Document 4: Amends the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Act of 1996 to permit coverage under title XIX (Medicaid)
Exempts such citizens from the five-year limited eligibility of qualified aliens for federal means-tested public benefits
Document 5: directs the Bureau of Labor Statistics to prepare and publish a monthly index for individuals who are 62 years of age or older
Document 6: establish a Social Security Surplus Protection Account to hold the Social Security surplus
Social Security Investment Commission to study the most effective vehicles for investment
Document 7: authorize waivers by the Commissioner of Social Security of the five-month waiting period for entitlement to benefits based on disability
undue hardship to terminally ill beneficiaries

topic.name: Educational Grants and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at providing grants and support for educational institutions and programs, particularly those serving underrepresented or disadvantaged populations.

Document 1: amends titles III and V of the Higher Education Act
permit an eligible minority-serving institution to concurrently receive grant funds
Document 2: awards competitive matching grants to partnerships
program targeting a high percentage of impoverished, struggling, or rural students
Document 3: authorize grants under the Teacher Quality Partnership grant program
training for current and prospective teachers
Document 4: awarding grants to nonprofit organizations that primarily serve veterans
rehabilitate and modify the primary residence of disabled or low-income veterans
Document 5: require the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services to award grants
expand medical residency training programs
Document 6: establish a Community Volunteer Income Tax Assistance Matching Grant Program
assist low-income taxpayers and members of underserved populations
Document 7: authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to local educational agencies
promote and strengthen education in subjects like music and arts

topic.name: Small Business Innovation and Technology Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at enhancing support for small businesses through innovation, technology development, and cybersecurity initiatives.

Document 1: Amends provisions of the Small Business Act relating to the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Program
Requires federal agencies, in making SBIR and STTR grant awards, to prioritize applications so as to increase the number of recipients from rural areas.
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations to the Secretary of Commerce for scientific and technical research and services
Establishes within NIST a Manufacturing Extension Partnership (MEP) Advisory Board.
Document 3: Establishes in the Executive Office of the President an Office of the Federal Chief Technology Officer (FCTO)
Promote technological innovation in the federal government.
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Act to authorize the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make grants to small business development centers (SBDCs)
Counsel their small business clients on cybersecurity.
Document 5: Requires government data assets made available by federal agencies to be published as machine-readable data
Agencies may engage with outside organizations and citizens to leverage public data assets for innovation.
Document 6: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to the authority of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to use certain SBA programs
Directs the SBA to report annually to specified congressional committees regarding all entrepreneurial development activities undertaken.
Document 7: Directs the Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) to provide federal science agencies with guidance on establishing specified policies
Requires NSF to publish a statistical summary of that data.

topic.name: Oil and Gas Development Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the exploration, leasing, and production of oil and gas resources in the United States, particularly in coastal and offshore areas.

Document 1: Authorizes exploration, leasing, development, and production of oil and gas from the Western Coastal Plain.
Directs the Secretary to establish lease sale procedures.
Document 2: Authorizes TransCanada Keystone Pipeline, L.P. to construct, connect, operate, and maintain pipeline facilities for the import of crude oil.
Declares that no permit pursuant to Executive Order 13337 shall be required for such Pipeline.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to implement a leasing program that includes at least 50% of the available unleased acreage.
Requires the Secretary to develop and submit a new five-year oil and gas leasing program.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish, implement, and administer a competitive oil and gas leasing program on the Coastal Plain of Alaska.
Repeals the prohibition against production or leasing of oil and gas resources from the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge.
Document 5: Authorizes the Bureau of Ocean Energy Management to conduct offshore oil and gas lease sales through Internet-based bidding methods.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue regulations to establish a specified graduated annual production incentive fee.
Prohibits the Secretary from issuing new oil or natural gas production leases in the Gulf of Mexico.
Document 7: Deems a domestic mine that will provide strategic and critical minerals to be an 'infrastructure project'.
Requires the lead agency to determine that any action to approve an exploration or mine permit does not constitute a major federal action.

topic.name: Regulatory Acts on Safety and Commerce
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various regulatory acts aimed at ensuring safety and proper commerce practices in areas such as children's products, controlled substances, firearms, food safety, wildlife protection, and marine conservation.

Document 1: bans as a hazardous substance
prohibits the manufacture, sale, or distribution in commerce of jewelry containing cadmium, barium, or antimony for children 12 years old or younger.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to direct the court to impose a sentence for specified controlled substance offenses
to reduce mandatory minimum sentences for manufacturing, distributing, dispensing, possessing, importing, or exporting specified controlled substances.
Document 3: amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to repeal the deferral until sale of the duty on large yachts
subject to certain conditions.
Document 4: makes it unlawful for anyone whose federal license to import, manufacture, or deal in firearms has been revoked
to transfer business inventory firearms into a personal collection.
Document 5: direct the Secretary of Agriculture to order an immediate halt to the distribution of meat
establish a program providing schools that participate in the school lunch or breakfast programs with training regarding the recall of meat.
Document 6: prohibits any person from importing, exporting, transporting, selling, receiving, acquiring, purchasing, breeding, possessing, or owning any prohibited wildlife species
includes among exemptions to such prohibition the breeding of such species by authorized persons.
Document 7: prohibits any person from offering billfish or billfish products for sale
subjects a person to a maximum civil penalty of $100,000 for each violation.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration and Security Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts related to immigration, refugee resettlement, and national security measures in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the use of federal funds for positions within U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE).
Document 2: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require the President to recommend the number of refugees admitted into the U.S. in a fiscal year.
Department of Homeland Security shall consult with the Director of National Intelligence and the Federal Bureau of Investigation.
Document 5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make it a crime to transmit the location of law enforcement agents while intending to further a federal crime relating to U.S. immigration.

topic.name: Higher Education Financial Assistance
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving access to and management of financial assistance for students in higher education.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require each lender entering into an educational loan arrangement with a postsecondary school to report annually to the Secretary of Education specified information concerning such arrangement.
Bars lenders or guarantors of educational loans from offering gifts to school employees or agents.
Document 2: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to repeal a provision which makes persons convicted of drug offenses ineligible to receive federal student financial assistance for specified periods.
Requires institutions of higher education to notify students who lost eligibility for such assistance due to a drug conviction that they are now eligible.
Document 3: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 by requiring the Department of Education to rank institutions of higher education based on the percentage of first-time, full-time students who receive Pell Grants.
Establishes consumer warning requirements for institutions with low enrollment or graduation rates.
Document 4: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to establish new programs for teacher student loan forgiveness under the guaranteed loan program.
Makes eligible for such student loan forgiveness any new borrower who is employed as a full-time teacher in a public elementary or secondary school in an economically disadvantaged area.
Document 5: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to allow a borrower of a Federal Direct Stafford Loan to elect a one-year grace period before beginning repayment on the loan.
The Department of Education must pay to each borrower an amount equal to the amount of interest that accrued on the loan prior to the beginning of the repayment period.
Document 6: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to provide grants to Predominantly Black Institutions to enhance their capacity to serve more low and middle-income African-American students.
Encourages such students to prepare for college and persist in secondary and postsecondary education.
Document 7: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to direct the Secretary of Education to establish a program to refinance the unpaid principal, accrued unpaid interest, and late charges on Federal Direct Loans.
Sets the interest rate on the refinanced loans at the rate for the 12 months beginning on July 1, 2013.

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Development
topic.description: This cluster of documents pertains to various legislative measures related to the management, development, and regulation of federal lands and resources in the United States.

Document 1: Adds the Tennessee counties of Giles, Hickman, Lincoln, Perry, and Wayne to the definition of 'Appalachian region' for purposes of Appalachian regional development efforts.
Document 3: Authorizes the disposal or use of the Winslow Hospitality Park located in Winslow, Navajo County, Arizona, for purposes other than public recreation.
Document 4: Directs the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) to reinstate the license and extend for three years the time period during which the licensee is required to commence the construction of project works.
Document 5: Requires the Administrator of the General Services Administration (GSA) to convey all right, title, and interest of the United States in and to the GSA Fleet Management Center located in Anchorage, Alaska.
Document 6: This bill takes into trust for the benefit of the Susanville Indian Rancheria approximately 301 acres of federal land under the administrative jurisdiction of the Bureau of Land Management.
Document 7: This bill provides that the Vanceboro Dam Storage Project, West Branch Project, and Forest City Project are not required to be licensed by the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission.

topic.name: Energy Regulation and Environmental Protection
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to energy production, environmental protection, and regulatory frameworks governing various energy sources.

Document 1: prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from issuing a permit or other authorization for exploration for or production of oil and gas
require the President to issue regulations to require owners or operators of offshore facilities to have their plans for responding to a worst case discharge of oil or a hazardous substance approved
Document 2: Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from taking any action under the Clean Air Act with respect to any stationary source permitting requirement
no action taken by the Administrator before the end of such period shall be considered to make carbon dioxide or methane a pollutant subject to regulation under the CAA
Document 3: Exempts projects determined by the Bureau of Land Management or the Forest Service to be meteorological site testing and monitoring projects from environmental impact statement requirements
Requires the BLM Director or Chief of the Forest Service to decide whether to issue a permit for such a project within 30 days after receiving an application
Document 4: require as a condition of each license issued for a commercial or industrial nuclear production or utilization facility that in the case of an unplanned release the licensee notify the Nuclear Regulatory Commission
the NRC to establish and maintain on its website a database of all notifications received by it of such unplanned releases
Document 5: prohibit exporting natural gas from the United States to a foreign country without first having secured an order of the Secretary of Energy
requires the Secretary to issue an environmental impact statement under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 on such an order
Document 6: require a new coal-fired electric generating unit to meet a standard of performance that, at a minimum, requires the unit to produce not more than 285 pounds of carbon dioxide per megawatt-hour
carbon dioxide that is injected into a geological formation shall not be counted as emissions discharged from a unit
Document 7: directs the Secretary of Energy to study and report to Congress with recommendations for appropriate methods and standards for requiring motor vehicle fuel suppliers to disclose country-of-origin information
authorizes the Secretary to impose a civil penalty of up to $10,000 on any person that knowingly violates such regulations

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming U.S. immigration policies, including adjustments to visa processes, asylum claims, and enforcement mechanisms.

Document 1: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to authorize the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to admit into the United States a qualifying Canadian citizen over 50 years old
Revises the secure travel partnership program (the visa waiver program as renamed by this Act)
Document 2: Provides permanent resident status adjustment for an applicant alien who was lawfully present in the United States on September 10, 2001
Waives specified grounds of inadmissibility.
Document 3: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to require inclusion of encrypted machine-readable electronic identification strips on Social Security cards
Prohibits individuals from commencing employment with a U.S. employer absent a Social Security card that meets the requirements of this Act.
Document 4: Authorizes states or their political subdivisions to enact and enforce immigration criminal penalties
Directs the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to provide the National Crime Information Center (NCIC) of the Department of Justice (DOJ) with all information that DHS has regarding any alien.
Document 5: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to eliminate the one-year time limit for filing an asylum claim
Establishes procedures to ensure the accuracy of statements taken by DHS employees exercising expedited removal authority.
Document 6: Eliminates the special repatriation requirements for unaccompanied alien children
An immigration officer who finds an inadmissible UAC at a land border or port of entry shall return the child to his or her country.
Document 7: Amends the William Wilberforce Trafficking Victims Protection Reauthorization Act of 2008 to declare that any unaccompanied alien child (UAC) who has not been a victim of a severe form of trafficking in persons shall be placed in removal proceedings
Requires a UAC to be placed within seven days of initial screening in a proceeding to expedite due process and screening.

topic.name: Employment and Unemployment Assistance
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing support and assistance to unemployed individuals and enhancing employment opportunities.

Document 1: employment assistance voucher program
subsidy from the state for the wages paid to the employee
Document 2: requirements for estimates of potential old-age insurance benefits
indicate in benefits estimates the percentage reduction in payment level
Document 3: exemption from employment taxes for the period
business tax credit for each worker retained
Document 4: penalty-free distributions to unemployed individuals from retirement plans
received federal or state unemployment compensation
Document 5: revising the formula for Tier-1 amounts
extends the period of emergency unemployment compensation
Document 6: amends the Family and Medical Leave Act
participate in or attend activities that are sponsored by a school
Document 7: repeals certain provisions which require the offset
annuity eligibility to a surviving spouse

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Security
topic.description: The documents collectively address various aspects of U.S. foreign policy, particularly in relation to diplomatic efforts, sanctions, and security measures in the Middle East and other regions.

Document 1: Directs the President to implement a regional diplomatic plan in conjunction with the U.N. Security Council to curtail the ongoing civil war in Iraq.
promote a national process of reconciliation within Iraq.
Document 2: Expresses the sense of Congress that diplomatic efforts to address Iran's illicit nuclear efforts are more likely to be effective if the President is empowered with explicit authority to impose additional sanctions.
additional funding should be provided to the Secretary of State to document and disseminate information about human rights abuses in Iran.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Defense to establish a strategy to prevent the proliferation of weapons of mass destruction and related materials in the Middle East and North Africa region.
establishment of appropriate metrics for determining success in the region.
Document 5: directs the Department of Energy to notify Congress within five days after receiving an application to transfer nuclear technology to China.
the President shall determine if China has violated the U.S.-China Nuclear Cooperation Agreement.

topic.name: Military Health Care and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at improving health care, mental health services, and support for members of the Armed Forces and their families.

Document 1: establish a Department of Defense (DOD)-wide patient navigator program
improve health care outcomes
Document 2: ensure that every member of the Armed Forces who has served in the Global War on Terror undergoes a physical examination
transmit a copy of a member's service medical record to the Secretary of Veterans Affairs
Document 3: design of a spouse-of-a-combat-veteran lapel button
present the button to such a spouse
Document 4: entitles any recovering member and any former member with a severe injury or illness to medical and dental care
authorize the Secretary to waive any limitation on the provision of such care
Document 5: provide a person-to-person mental health assessment for each member of the Armed Forces
report on an evaluation of specific tools, processes, and best practices to improve the identification of mental health conditions
Document 6: treat as a dependent child of such veteran any unmarried person who is placed in the legal custody of the veteran
determine whether a veteran is unable to defray the necessary expenses of hospital, nursing home, and domiciliary care
Document 7: assign a medical care case manager and a service member advocate
establish a Department of Defense (DOD)-wide Ombudsman Office

topic.name: Child and Family Support Programs
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing child care services, supporting family caregivers, and improving public health through various federal initiatives.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish a business-incentive grant program to provide child care through public-private partnerships.
Gives priority in grant selection to businesses with fewer than 100 full-time employees.
Document 2: Amends part E (Federal Payments for Foster Care and Adoption Assistance) of title IV of the Social Security Act to give state plans the option of providing for the state to enter into agreements to provide kinship guardianship assistance payments.
Makes individuals with whom kinship guardianship assistance payments are being made categorically eligible for Medicaid.
Document 4: The Caregivers and Veterans Omnibus Health Services Act of 2010 is amended to extend through December 31, 2017, authority for the pilot program on assistance for child care for certain veterans receiving health care.
Authority for recovery from third parties of the cost of care and services furnished to veterans with health-plan contracts for non-service-connected disability is extended through FY2017.
Document 5: Requires the Secretary to establish the Public Health Workforce Scholarship Program to assure an adequate supply of public health professionals to eliminate critical public health workforce shortages.

Requires the Secretary to award grants to public health agencies that receive public health preparedness cooperative agreements from HHS to operate state, local, and tribal public health workforce loan repayment programs.
Document 6: Authorizes a qualified entity to use Medicare data, and information derived from service provider and supplier performance evaluations, for additional non-public analyses.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to provide Medicare claims data to qualified clinical data registries for purposes of linking it with clinical outcomes data.

topic.name: Legislative Reforms and Protections
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative reforms aimed at improving protections and rights for specific groups, including veterans, Native Americans, and whistleblowers.

1: Amends the Hate Crime Statistics Act to require the Attorney General to acquire data about crimes that manifest evidence of prejudice based on gender.
Hate Crime Statistics Improvement Act of 2009
2: Extends the 120-day limit for the filing of an appeal to the Court of Veterans Appeals after a final decision of the Board of Veterans' Appeals.
Fair Access to Veterans Benefits Act of 2010
3: Directs the Comptroller General to study and report to Congress on the performance of the federal government in meeting goals for participation in federal procurement contracts by small businesses owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans and women.
4: Amends the Department of Defense, Emergency Supplemental Appropriations to Address Hurricanes in the Gulf of Mexico, and Pandemic Influenza Act, 2006 to extend certain authorities in providing assistance to local educational agencies affected by Hurricane Katrina or Rita.
5: Repeals outdated provisions regarding treatment of Native Americans, including provisions on hostile tribes and penalties for truancy.
Repealing Existing Substandard Provisions Encouraging Conciliation with Tribes Act or the RESPECT Act
6: Declares that the right to life guaranteed by the Constitution is vested in each human being, encompassing all stages of life.
7: Extends personnel protections to whistleblowers by classifying the suspension or revocation of access to classified information as a personnel action.
National Security Whistleblower Protection Act of 2016

topic.name: Federal Accountability and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing transparency, accountability, and regulatory oversight within federal agencies and programs.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Funding Accountability and Transparency Act of 2006 to revise the contents of the existing single updated searchable public website.
Requires the website to include for all federal funding for each federal agency.
Document 2: Amends the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act to repeal the contingent conditions under which the National Association of Registered Agents and Brokers (NARAB) shall not be established.
Retains states' regulatory authority over licensing, supervision, disciplining, and setting of licensing fees for insurance producers.
Document 3: Amends the portion of the Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938 (FLSA) known as the Equal Pay Act to revise remedies for, enforcement of, and exceptions to prohibitions against sex discrimination in the payment of wages.
Prohibits retaliation for inquiring about, discussing, or disclosing the wages of the employee or another employee.
Document 4: Amends the National Housing Act to create the Refinance Program Oversight Board, which shall establish and oversee a program for insuring homeownership retention mortgages.
Instructs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) to insure any homeownership retention mortgage.
Document 5: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prescribe requirements with which a federal banking agency must comply in order to use an independent consultant in implementing a consent order.
Requires the agency to make specified information about the consent agreement and the independent consultant available to the public.
Document 6: Amends part E of title IV of the Social Security Act to require state child protection standards to ensure that the immigration status alone of a parent shall not disqualify the parent from being a placement for a child.
Requires the state plan for foster care and adoption assistance to notify relatives seeking placement of a child that their immigration status will not be questioned.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to require specified mortgagees to indemnify HUD for payment of a mortgage insurance claim if the mortgage was not materially originated or underwritten in accordance with HUD requirements.

Establishes within the Federal Housing Administration a Deputy Assistant Secretary for Risk Management and Regulatory Affairs responsible for managing and mitigating the risk to HUD mortgage insurance funds.

topic.name: Transportation and Climate Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on U.S. legislation aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions and enhancing transportation efficiency to address climate change.

Document 1: U.S. policy to develop a national surface transportation system that advances the national interest and defense, interstate and foreign commerce, and the protection of the environment.
increase the total usage of public transportation, intercity passenger rail services, and non-motorized transportation.
Document 2: Amends the Clean Air Act to set forth provisions concerning global warming pollution emissions.
require each fleet of automobiles sold by a manufacturer beginning in model year 2016 to meet emission standards.
Document 3: Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish a National Greenhouse Gas Database.
Establishes a program for the market-driven reduction of GHGs by covered entities through the use of tradeable emissions allowances.
Document 4: Establishes the Low Greenhouse Gas Transportation Fund.
Requires states and MPOs to establish goals for reducing greenhouse gas emissions from the transportation sector.
Document 5: Authorizes additional FY2011-FY2012 appropriations for formula grants to both urbanized and nonurbanized areas for the operating and capital costs of public transportation.
requires a 100% federal share of a grant project involving acquisition of clean fuel or alternative fuel vehicle-related equipment.
Document 6: Amends the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007 to require the Secretary of Energy to establish a program of research, development, demonstration, and commercial application for geothermal heat pumps.
make grants to state and local governments, institutions of higher education, nonprofit entities, utilities, and for-profit companies to promote the development of geothermal heat pumps.
Document 7: Establishes in the Department of Energy (DOE) a program to provide grants to eligible entities, on a competitive basis, to develop and carry out clean energy and carbon reduction measures.
decrease the tax deduction for oil related qualified production activities income of major integrated oil companies.

topic.name: Consumer Safety and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing consumer safety through regulation of products, medications, and food labeling.

Document 1: Requires the head of each federal product safety enforcement agency to design and implement a public information plan
Requires the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to establish a clearinghouse to collect and distribute product recall and safety information
Document 2: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to expand requirements for assessing the use of medications in pediatric populations
The FDA may require the sponsor of an approved medication that could be used to treat pediatric cancer to complete a pediatric assessment
Document 3: This bill establishes the Prescription Drug and Medical Device Price Review Board within the Food and Drug Administration
Manufacturers may not charge excessive prices
Document 4: Requires each contract entered into with a prescription drug plan (PDP) sponsor to prohibit the PDP from entering into a contract with any pharmacy benefits manager
Requires the PDP sponsor to establish a process to appeal, investigate, and resolve disputes regarding individual drug prices
Document 5: Requires the proposed rule issued by the Commissioner of Food and Drugs (FDA) pertaining to over-the-counter sunscreen drug products to take effect 180 days after enactment
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to implement a general, nationwide education campaign identifying the risks posed by sun exposure
Document 6: Requires the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to establish a national voluntary bioengineered food labeling standard
USDA must provide science-based information through education, outreach, and promotion to address consumer acceptance of agricultural biotechnology
Document 7: Amends the Consumer Product Safety Act to require the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to prescribe consumer product testing programs
Increases the actions the CPSC may take in ordering recalls of products presenting substantial hazards

topic.name: Healthcare Legislation for Small Businesses
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving healthcare coverage and insurance options for small businesses and their employees.

Document 1: Provides for the establishment and governance of Fully Funded Small Business Health Insurance cooperatives
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to establish the small business CHOICE tax credit for small employers
Document 2: Prohibits a group health plan from imposing an aggregate lifetime benefit limit of less than: $5 million for the first two plan years
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to contract with the Institute of Medicine for a study
Document 3: Requires an insurance issuer that offers a long-term care insurance plan to implement claims dispute resolution procedures
Allows an enrollee to appeal the decision of an independent reviewer to an appropriate state court
Document 4: Declares that the development, recognition, or implementation of any guideline under any provision of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act shall not be construed to establish the standard of care
No provision of PPACA shall be construed to preempt any state law governing medical professional liability cases
Document 5: Amends the Public Health Service Act to expand the 340B drug pricing program
Allows participation as a covered entity by a public or nonprofit entity that primarily provides family planning services
Document 6: Requires a group health plan to ensure inpatient coverage for breast cancer treatment
Prohibits such a plan from restricting benefits for any hospital length of stay to less than 48 hours
Document 7: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to transfer from the Prevention and Public Health Fund
Eliminates the eligibility requirement for the temporary high risk pool which prohibits an individual from having creditable coverage

topic.name: Government Housing and Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to housing, military construction, and employee benefits within government agencies.

Document 1: amends the United States Housing Act of 1937 to subject a small public housing agency (PHA) to the same requirements as a PHA
HUD shall evaluate the management of a small PHA's voucher program
Document 2: requires the Office of Personnel Management (OPM) to establish a Postal Service Health Benefits Program
To obtain or continue federal coverage, Medicare-eligible postal retirees must be enrolled in part A and part B
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to acquire real property and carry out military construction projects
Authorizes appropriations to the Army for fiscal years after 2007 for military construction, land acquisition, and military family housing functions
Document 4: Sets forth rules for the recalculation of annuities for employees of the U.S. Postal Service (USPS)
Eliminates the requirement for pre-funding of the Postal Service Retiree Health Benefits Fund
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to acquire real property and carry out military construction projects
Provides, with respect to the Navy, authorizations paralleling those provided for the Army
Document 6: extends for an additional eight fiscal years the State Small Business Credit Initiative
Treasury may award, on a competitive basis, up to a total of $1 billion in two tranches
Document 7: requires the head of each executive agency to establish a policy under which eligible employees may be authorized to telework
Requires each agency head to provide an interactive telework training program to eligible employees and their managers

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States, specifically regarding the temporary suspension of duties on various products.

Document 1: extends the temporary suspension of duty on certain porcelain lamp-holder housings.
Document 2: modify the article description of certain men's footwear, and (2) extend the temporary suspension of duty on such footwear.
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on nonenumerated footwear with textile uppers for women.
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on mixtures of Disperse Red 367, Benzo, and Acetic acid.
Document 5: suspend temporarily the duty on certain laminated rolled filmstock.
Document 6: extend the temporary suspension of duty on e-caprolactone-2-ethyl-2-(hydroxymethyl)-1,3-propanediol polymer.
Document 7: suspend temporarily the duty on certain work footwear for men covering the ankle.

topic.name: Public Health Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at improving public health services and addressing health-related issues in the United States.

Document 1: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to enter into an agreement with the Institute of Medicine to conduct a comprehensive study on gaps in mental health services.
Consideration of gaps in mental health treatment services for adults and children with severe and persistent mental illness.
Document 2: Reauthorizes the Young Women's Breast Health Education and Awareness Requires Learning Young Act of 2009 (EARLY Act).
Supports campaigns to educate the public and health care professionals about young women's breast health.
Document 3: Establishes and provides funds for the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) Emergency Response Fund.
Funds may be used to detect, prepare for, or respond to public health threats domestically and abroad.
Document 4: Directs the Director of the National Institute of Environmental Health Sciences to evaluate the health effects of housing-related health hazards.
Requires the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) to determine the data and resources needed to establish a healthy housing data collection system.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award grants to nonprofit organizations to increase the media literacy of girls and boys.
Supports the empowerment of girls or boys through extracurricular activities and programs.
Document 6: Establishes the National Health Workforce Advisory Council to make recommendations on health workforce issues.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make grants to states for ensuring access to health services for all individuals.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award grants to nonprofit organizations to provide for the establishment, operation, coordination, and evaluation of programs to increase media literacy.
Directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to convene a task force for promoting healthy and positive depictions of girls and women in the media.

topic.name: Native American Land and Recognition Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative acts that address land rights, federal recognition, and resource management for Native American tribes in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to take into trust two tracts of federal land identified as tract B and tract D that were historically part of the Albuquerque Indian School for the benefit of the 19 New Mexico Indian Pueblos.
Prohibits any gaming activities from being carried out on such land taken into trust.
Document 2: Authorizes Sealaska, the regional Alaska Native Corporation for southeast Alaska, to select and receive conveyance of its remaining land entitlement under the Alaska Native Claims Settlement Act.
Includes certain mapped sites that have traditional, recreational, and renewable energy use value.
Document 3: Extends federal recognition to the Muscogee Nation of Florida and makes the Nation and its members eligible for all services and benefits provided by the federal government to federally recognized Indian tribes.
Authorizes the Secretary to take land in trust on behalf of the Nation.
Document 6: This bill amends the Act of June 7, 1956, to extend federal recognition to the Lumbee Tribe of North Carolina and make its members eligible for the services and benefits provided to members of federally recognized tribes.
The Department of the Interior may take land into trust for the tribe.

topic.name: Legislative Accountability and Reform
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing accountability, transparency, and integrity in the U.S. federal government and its processes.

Document 1: Amends the Congressional Budget Act of 1974 to require macroeconomic impact analysis of major bills.
Defines 'major bill or resolution' based on its budgetary effects on U.S. GDP.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act to restrict political involvement of state election officials.
Document 3: Requires audit of Senate earmarks and improves transparency of earmark requests.
Amends Senate rules regarding directed spending provisions.
Document 4: Prohibits misleading Congress regarding the use of military force by the executive branch.
Imposes penalties for falsifying information to Congress.
Document 5: Prohibits federal agencies from finalizing 'midnight rules' with significant economic impact.
Defines 'midnight rule' and establishes a moratorium period.
Document 6: Revises requirements for federal agency rulemaking to enhance accountability.
Expands scope of judicial review of agency rulemaking.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to create a system for campaign contribution vouchers.
Establishes oversight for a pilot program to enhance electoral participation.

topic.name: Military Sexual Assault and Violence Response
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislative measures aimed at preventing and responding to sexual assault and domestic violence within the military.

Document 1: Directs each military department Secretary to implement a program providing a Special Victims' Counsel to a victim of a sexual assault committed by a member of the Armed Forces.
Requires such victim to be informed of the availability of such assistance at the time the victim originally seeks assistance.
Document 4: Establishes in the Department of Defense an Office of the Victims' Advocate to facilitate access to services for victims of domestic or family violence, sexual assault, and stalking in the military.
Specifies the rights of victims of such violence reported to a military law enforcement agency.

topic.name: Medicare and Medicaid Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving healthcare services and equity within the Medicare and Medicaid systems.

Document 1: establish additional requirements for assigning Medicare fee-for-service beneficiaries to accountable care organizations (ACOs)
enables ACOs to receive payments for savings stemming from care coordination and management.
Document 2: amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
establish a breast cancer treatment quality performance system
Document 3: establish a demonstration project under which a state shall reimburse
emergency medical condition.
Document 4: cover a Voluntary Advance Care Planning Consultation
make grants to eligible entities to establish statewide programs
Document 5: cover certified adult day services furnished in a certified adult day services center
require the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to increase by 3% the payment amount
Document 6: redistribute unused residency positions for which graduate medical education costs are paid under Medicare
increase the resident limit for each qualifying hospital that applies for an increase.
Document 7: prohibit payment at the applicable site neutral payment rate for discharges
study the treatment needs of individuals entitled to benefits under Medicare part A

topic.name: Consumer Credit Protection Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating consumer credit practices to protect consumers from unfair lending practices and fees.

Document 1: Prohibit a creditor from extending credit to a consumer under an open end consumer credit plan for which the fee and interest rate exceeds 36%.
Empowers state Attorneys General to enforce this Act.
Document 3: Limit the annual percentage rate (APR) applicable to any consumer credit transaction to the maximum rate permitted by the laws of the state in which the consumer resides.
Document 5: Prohibit a creditor from imposing a fee based on the manner in which payment on the account is made.

topic.name: Mine and Pipeline Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing safety and environmental protection in mining and pipeline operations.

Document 1: Robert C. Byrd Mine Safety Protection Act of 2011
requires the Secretary of Labor to determine why an accident occurred and whether there were violations of law
Document 2: Pipeline Infrastructure and Community Protection Act of 2011
prescribes or revises requirements for safety and environmental protection in pipeline transportation
Document 3: Strengthening Pipeline Safety and Enforcement Act of 2010
defines the term 'major consequence violation' to mean a violation that contributed to any incident resulting in deaths or injuries
Document 4: Oil Spill Prevention Act of 2008
requires all new U.S. vessels to comply with regulations requiring double hulls
Document 6: Pipeline Transportation Safety Improvement Act of 2011
subjects to a civil penalty of $250,000 any person that the Secretary of Transportation has found to have committed a major consequence violation

topic.name: Housing and Support Services Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving access to housing and support services for vulnerable populations, including youth, the elderly, and low-income families.

Document 1: Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to include local educational agency homeless liaisons...
Requires postsecondary schools to designate a staff person as a single point of contact to assist homeless and foster care youth.
Document 3: Establishes in the Department of Housing and Urban Development a program to provide assistance for elderly relatives...
Provides such assistance in the form of financing for the construction, reconstruction, moderate or substantial rehabilitation...
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to make grants and loans to qualified states...
Requires such program to be administered through HUD's Office of Community Planning and Development.

topic.name: Tax Reform Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at reforming the tax code and addressing tax-related issues in the United States.

Document 1: Makes permanent the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001
Repeals the estate tax and the tax on generation-skipping transfers.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend provisions allowing deferral of tax on gain from the sale of employer securities to an S corporation-sponsored ESOP
Allows an estate tax deduction for 50% of the proceeds from the sale of employer securities to an S corporation-sponsored ESOP.
Document 3: Grants a preference to Patriot corporations in the evaluation of bids for federal contracts
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to reduce the income tax rate for Patriot corporations.
Document 4: Imposes an excise tax on payments made pursuant to U.S. government contracts
Expresses the sense of the Senate that tax revenues raised should be used for public financing of Senate elections.
Document 5: Requires an individual taxpayer whose adjusted gross income exceeds $1 million to pay a minimum tax rate of 30%
Expresses the sense of the Senate that Congress should enact tax reform that repeals unfair tax loopholes.
Document 6: Treats gain or loss from the sale of oil or natural gas as ordinary income or loss
Directs the Secretary of the Treasury to study the tax treatment of certain commodities.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code regarding the classification of workers for employment tax purposes
Directs the Secretary to issue regulations clarifying the proper employment status of workers.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at modifying tax deductions, credits, and rates for individuals and corporations.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow bona fide residents of the District of Columbia an exclusion from gross income
for income derived from sources within the District of Columbia.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual taxpayers a deduction from gross income for interest paid on indebtedness
for the purchase of a motor vehicle.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to modify the tax deduction for charitable contributions of food inventory
make such deduction permanent.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the tax deduction for mortgage insurance premiums.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to revise the income tax rates on corporations
impose a single 20% rate on corporate taxable income.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the new markets tax credit
provide for an inflation adjustment to the limitation amount for such credit.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow 100% expensing in a current taxable year of the cost of an automated fire sprinkler system
accelerated depreciation of such an automated fire sprinkler system.

topic.name: Agricultural and Economic Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to agricultural disaster assistance, housing finance reform, and economic sanctions that aim to promote economic stability and development.

Document 1: establish a program of supplemental agricultural disaster assistance
Agricultural Disaster Relief Trust Fund to provide payments to farmers and ranchers
Document 4: Amends the National Housing Act to modify requirements for the maximum principal loan obligation
revises eligibility criteria for cash down payment for Federal Housing Administration (FHA) mortgage insurance
Document 5: increasing the competitiveness of U.S. manufacturers will strengthen the national economy
require state or local workforce investment systems to use youth activities funds allocated to a local area for programs that provide training

topic.name: Legislative Measures and Designations
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative measures related to the designation of federal facilities and amendments to existing laws.

Document 1: Amends the Agricultural Adjustment Act to prohibit the Secretary of Agriculture from using distance or transportation costs from a location not within a marketing area as the basis for pricing Class I (fluid) milk.
Federal Milk Marketing Reform Act of 2009.
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 2551 Galena Avenue in Simi Valley, California, as the 'Neil Havens Post Office.'
Document 3: Amends the federal judicial code to provide for an additional judgeship for the western district of Michigan.
Document 4: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 13500 SW 250 Street in Princeton, Florida, as the 'Corporal Christian A. Guzman Rivera Post Office Building.'
Document 5: Designates the federal building located at 617 Walnut Street in Helena, Arkansas, as the 'Jacob Trieber Federal Building, United States Post Office, and United States Court House.'
Document 6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 3031 Veterans Road West in Staten Island, New York, as the 'Leonard Montalto Post Office Building.'
Document 7: Accepts the request of the Miami Tribe of Oklahoma to revoke the charter of incorporation issued to that tribe.

topic.name: Legislative Acts and Amendments
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts and amendments aimed at addressing infrastructure, health, education, and financial regulations.

Document 1: Frank Buckles World War I Memorial Act
establish a commemorative work rededicating the District of Columbia War Memorial
Document 2: National Infrastructure Bank Act of 2007
establish an independent National Infrastructure Bank
Document 3: Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act
claims presented to an Indian Health Service contracting officer
Document 4: Older Americans HIV Information and Prevention Act of 2010
includes routine screening, prevention and mitigation programs for HIV
Document 5: amends the Workforce Investment Act of 1998
permit the establishment of Job Corps centers in outlying areas
Document 6: amends the Internal Revenue Code
forfeiture of any amount covered into the treasury
Document 7: repeals the Consumer Financial Protection Act of 2010
revives or restores the provisions of law amended by such Act
